Title: Decoding the Multifunctionality of B-Box Proteins: Bridging Light, Stress and Developmental Networks in Diverse Plant Species

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: B-box zinc-finger protein, Group IV BBX with two B-box domains and no CCT domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates HY5 transcription and promotes HY5 downstream flavonoid genes such as CHS
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; protein interaction with HY5 module
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive BBX regulator of UV-B-induced flavonoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary based on cited functional studies including promoter binding, interaction assays, and metabolite/pigment analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BBX21 activates HY5 transcription. 2. It promotes CHS expression and UV-B-induced flavonoid biosynthesis. 3. It functions as a positive regulator in the BBX-HY5 light signaling module.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Positive target to enhance light-induced pigmentation and antioxidant flavonoids
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also regulates photomorphogenesis; core metabolite relevance is through CHS/flavonoid induction

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: HY5-mediated CHS and other flavonoid pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Synergistically enhances HY5-mediated expression of CHS and other flavonoid genes under UV-B
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator in UV-B flavonoid response
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited genetic and expression studies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BBX31 has light-quality dependent dual function. 2. Under UV-B it enhances HY5-mediated flavonoid gene expression. 3. It supports UV-protective pigment accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for inducible UV-protective flavonoid enhancement
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function is partly redundant with BBX21 and light-quality dependent

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: B-box zinc-finger protein, Group IV BBX
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin biosynthesis; JA-mediated stress-related metabolic regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates IbPRX17 and represses IbJAZ10, thereby relieving inhibition on MYC2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Reported BBX regulator modulating anthocyanin biosynthesis in sweet potato in this review context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Modulates anthocyanin biosynthesis and stress-associated ROS/JA responses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited promoter binding, cooperative regulation, and stress phenotype analyses
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbBBX24 activates ROS scavenging genes with IbTOE3. 2. It represses IbJAZ10 to enhance JA signaling. 3. The review also notes BBX24 involvement in anthocyanin biosynthesis in sweet potato.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful multi-trait target for pigmentation and stress resilience engineering
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins contribute pigmentation, antioxidant capacity, and stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review emphasizes both stress and pigment roles; direct anthocyanin targets are less detailed here than stress targets

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CHS and UFGT in complex with HY5
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator promoting pear anthocyanin accumulation under light
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes red pigmentation and anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited complex formation, target activation, and pigment assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcBBX18 forms a complex with HY5. 2. The complex activates CHS and UFGT. 3. This promotes anthocyanin accumulation in pear.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving fruit color and nutritional anthocyanin content

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CHS; UFGT promoter regions via competition with PcBBX18-HY5 module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses pigment production by competitively antagonizing PcBBX18-HY5 activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces anthocyanin accumulation and pigment production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited promoter competition and pigmentation analyses
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcBBX21 antagonizes PcBBX18-HY5 activation. 2. It represses anthocyanin biosynthesis under low light. 3. It reduces pear pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing or downregulation may enhance pear coloration

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly upregulates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes in a light-dependent manner
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of sweet cherry fruit pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases anthocyanin accumulation and fruit coloration
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and gene expression/pigment analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavBBX6 upregulates CHS and UFGT. 2. Overexpression increases anthocyanin accumulation. 3. It enhances fruit skin pigmentation in a light-dependent manner.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for fruit color biofortification
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented together with PavBBX9 as similar positive regulators

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly upregulates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes in a light-dependent manner
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of sweet cherry fruit pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases anthocyanin accumulation and fruit coloration
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and gene expression/pigment analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavBBX9 upregulates CHS and UFGT. 2. Overexpression increases anthocyanin accumulation. 3. It enhances fruit skin pigmentation in a light-dependent manner.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for fruit color biofortification
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented together with PavBBX6 as similar positive regulators

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes flavonoid biosynthetic gene activation through interaction with HY5
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: light-responsive transcriptional activation via HY5 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of UV-B-induced fruit coloration
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances UV-B-responsive pigmentation and flavonoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from interaction studies, expression analyses, and pigment assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdBBX22 interacts with HY5. 2. It promotes UV-B-induced flavonoid/anthocyanin accumulation. 3. Its expression is light responsive and fruit skin specific.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for improving apple peel coloration and antioxidant quality
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review emphasizes tight spatial and environmental regulation

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Loss-of-function allele enhances pigmentation, implying PyBBX24 normally represses or limits anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: negative regulatory control inferred from natural loss-of-function allele
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Natural allele with strong effect on color intensity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function increases red pigmentation and also reduces plant height
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Natural allelic variation analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A 14-bp deletion creates a loss-of-function PyBBX24 allele. 2. This allele enhances red coloration. 3. It also causes dwarfism/early flowering, indicating pleiotropy.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value non-transgenic allele for pear color improvement, but pleiotropy must be managed
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced plant height; pleiotropic developmental effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Species designation in review is Pyrus; exact species/cultivar not specified

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Contributes positively to anthocyanin and proanthocyanidin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Branch-level BBX regulator affecting multiple flavonoid products
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited functional study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtrBBX23 contributes to anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. It also promotes proanthocyanidin accumulation. 3. It highlights BBX control across multiple flavonoid branches.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool to modulate multiple antioxidant flavonoid branches
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents broader BBX role beyond anthocyanins alone

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Regulates carotenoid biosynthesis in tomato fruit
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Species-specific BBX regulator for tomato carotenoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes carotenoid biosynthesis linked to fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited tomato functional studies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlBBX20 regulates carotenoid biosynthesis in tomato. 2. This role is highlighted as species-specific and important for fruit ripening. 3. It links BBX function to nutritional carotenoid traits.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator to improve tomato carotenoid nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also discussed elsewhere in review for flowering transition, indicating pleiotropy
